questions.scenario: A customer reports checkout failed once at 09:42 UTC. Application Insights has operation ID `checkout-5001`, request `POST /checkout`, result `500`, and a SQL dependency timeout. What is the best way to explain the incident?

options.text: Follow the operation ID through request, dependency, exception, and trace evidence.
Read only the final exception text, because the exception is always the full story.
Start with dashboard colors, because a red chart explains one customer request.
Ignore dependencies, because backend APIs fail only inside their own process.

questions.explanation: Application Insights is strongest when the team follows one operation across requests, dependencies, exceptions, and traces. The final exception is useful, but it is only one part of the story.

questions.scenario: The request succeeded, but users say checkout felt slow. Application Insights shows `POST /checkout` took 1400 ms and the receipt Blob write took 90 ms. Which evidence should the team compare next?

options.text: Dependency durations for the same operation, because a slow downstream call can make a successful request feel slow.
Action group email receivers, because receiver names explain slow calls.
The resource group location only, because location fully explains every slow request.
The current article title, because the UI route might be stale.

questions.explanation: Dependency timing helps explain where request time went. The request can succeed while one downstream call consumes most of the latency.
